name: Stacked stone corner
description: Reinforced building corner formed by a vertical stack of large, irregular cobblestone blocks that runs the full height of the wall and reads visually heavier than the adjoining surface. The corner sits one block wider than the wall plane so it projects slightly into the facade, making the boundary between the two perpendicular wall runs explicit and giving the volume a perceptible structural anchor. Applied to all four corners of a rectangular footprint, it frames the building like quoining on a real masonry cottage and lets the lighter @main_wall material (smooth stone, plaster, planks) sit safely between the heavy stone piers. Typical of medieval cottages, rustic mountain houses, barns, and small castle outbuildings where rough quoins articulate humble walls.
